judgment.worst_element: The objective states 'Retrieve the Tectonic Heart' but it's listed as an encounter 2 pickup (passive defense item) — the item's role is inconsistent between the objective and the level design.
judgment.overall: A well-designed medium-difficulty level with excellent thematic coherence and mechanical sense; the minor objective inconsistency is the only design issue worth flagging.

completeness.reason: Locations are distinct. Enemies are varied. Encounter 1 and 0 share the same damage value (5) — a minor escalation gap. 'Falling petals' as a hazard in enc 2 is the most distinctive hazard in a serene context. No placeholder text.

judgment.best_element: The Guardian Crest from encounter 2 explicitly 'Provides shield against boss damage' — the only pickup in the dataset that is transparently designed for the boss fight, giving the player a clear tool.
judgment.worst_element: Zephyr Feather (movement speed) as a combat weakness for a Mist Stalker lacks mechanical logic — speed buffs don't obviously harm a predator enemy.
judgment.overall: A clean, well-referenced easy-difficulty level that would be fun to play; the speed-as-weapon weakness and flat enc 0-1 damage values are minor design issues in an otherwise solid configuration.

judgment.summary: An ominous jungle necromancer level where encounter 2 enemy (Shadow Jaguar) is weak to 'Scythe of Withering' — the player weapon — rather than enc 1's Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger. The boss correctly uses Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger. The player weapon is used as a weakness in two different encounters.

reference_integrity.reason: Enc 0 weakness 'Scythe of Withering' (player weapon) exact. Enc 1 weakness 'Corpse-Lantern' (enc 0 pickup) exact. Enc 2 weakness 'Scythe of Withering' — reverts to player weapon; enc 1 pickup was 'Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger'. Boss weakness 'Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger' (enc 1 pickup) exact. One chain break.

causal_chain.reason: Enc 0 trigger 'Light the Corpse-Lantern to reveal the path to the altar' — this is using the pickup, not defeating the enemy. Enc 1 'Slay the Ape to activate the ancient stone gate' — defeat-triggered. Enc 2 'Defeat the Jaguar to unlock the sanctum entrance' — defeat-triggered. One trigger is a pickup-use action.

balance.reason: Health escalates 40-60-80 and damage 10-15-20. Boss at 150/25 strictly harder. Total incoming (10+15+20+25=70) vs 100 HP — appropriate for hard? Actually this seems like medium difficulty numbers for a hard label. Damage escalation is modest. Essence Vials (20 HP) provides minimal healing.

thematic_coherence.reason: Sunken Amazonian Rainforest, Rotting Vine-Stalker, Carrion Ape, Shadow Jaguar, Grave Warden of the Canopy — all reinforce dark necromantic overgrowth. Corpse-Lantern, Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger — excellent necromancer-specific items for a jungle setting.

mechanical_sense.reason: Corpse-Lantern (increases attack range) against a Carrion Ape — necromantic range advantage over a melee ape is plausible. Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger (stuns on strike) against the Grave Warden is logical — stunning a guardian to purify the blighted heart. Boss victory condition 'Purify the Blighted Heart' doesn't name the item, though.

completeness.reason: Boss victory condition 'Purify the Blighted Heart' names no specific item — generic. Enc 0 trigger uses pickup rather than defeat. Encounter 2 weapon reversion. Three completeness concerns. Locations are distinct.

judgment.best_element: Enemy naming — Rotting Vine-Stalker, Carrion Ape, Shadow Jaguar, Grave Warden of the Canopy — creates a coherent dark jungle bestiary that feels designed around the necromancer class.
judgment.worst_element: Boss victory condition 'Purify the Blighted Heart' provides no instruction on using the stated boss weakness item (Soul-Infused Obsidian Dagger) — players would not know how to end the fight.
judgment.overall: Strong thematic design with an evocative setting, but multiple structural gaps (enc 0 trigger, enc 2 weapon reversion, generic boss condition) require revision before this level is shippable.

judgment.summary: A whimsical neon jungle alchemist level where encounter 1 enemy weakness is 'Dewdrop Essence coating' rather than the exact pickup name 'Dewdrop Essence'. Otherwise references are clean. The Gilded Toad King is a memorable boss name and the victory condition is specific.

reference_integrity.reason: Enc 0 weakness 'Alchemical Acid Flask' (player weapon) exact. Enc 1 weakness 'Dewdrop Essence coating' — adds 'coating' qualifier; pickup is 'Dewdrop Essence'. Minor variation but identifiable. Enc 2 weakness 'Ever-Flowing Mana Vials' (enc 1 pickup) exact. Boss weakness 'Ever-Flowing Mana Vials' exact, victory condition names it exactly.

causal_chain.reason: Enc 0 'Defeating the sprite reveals the path to the Glowing Riverbank' — defeat-triggered. Enc 1 'Capturing the monkey opens the vine-covered temple entrance' — defeat/capture triggered. Enc 2 'Shattering the guardian grants access to the throne room' — defeat-triggered. All three are defeat or capture consequences.

balance.reason: Health escalates 20-30-40 and damage 5-8-10. Boss at 50/10 strictly harder. Total incoming (5+8+10+10=33) vs 100 HP — trivially easy, appropriate. Alchemist's Satchel (max potion capacity) from enc 2 is a useful alchemist-specific buff.

thematic_coherence.reason: Neon Jungle, Vine-Tangled Sprite, Mud-Slinging Monkey, Petrified Tree Guardian, Gilded Toad King — every element is consistently whimsical jungle fantasy. Dewdrop Essence, Ever-Flowing Mana Vials, Alchemist's Satchel — class-appropriate whimsical items.

mechanical_sense.reason: Dewdrop Essence (health regen) coating a monkey to damage it is a stretch. Ever-Flowing Mana Vials (unlocks mana harnessing) against a Petrified Tree Guardian and the Toad King — the alchemist using mana to restore the boss's true form is thematically appropriate for a whimsical alchemist.

completeness.reason: All locations are distinct. Enemies are unique and personality-driven. Every pickup effect is specific. Boss victory condition explicitly states the mechanic. No placeholder text.

judgment.best_element: The boss victory condition — 'Splash the Toad King with the Ever-Flowing Mana Vials to restore his true form' — is both mechanically specific and tonally perfect for a whimsical easy-difficulty level.
judgment.worst_element: Dewdrop Essence 'coating' qualifier in encounter 1 weakness field; a minor imprecision, but exact naming would make this entry flawless.
judgment.overall: A charming, well-constructed easy-difficulty level with excellent thematic consistency; one minor reference qualifier and a slightly stretched mechanical connection are the only issues.

judgment.summary: An eerie jungle chronomancer level with clean exact references. The causal trigger in encounter 1 describes the player 'capturing the monkey's spirit' rather than defeating it, which is slightly ambiguous but thematically appropriate. Boss correctly uses Temporal Siphon.

reference_integrity.reason: Enc 0 weakness 'Time Blade' (player weapon) exact. Enc 1 weakness 'Chronos Shard' (enc 0 pickup) exact. Enc 2 weakness 'Temporal Siphon' (enc 1 pickup) exact. Boss weakness 'Temporal Siphon' exact, victory condition names it exactly.

causal_chain.reason: Enc 0 'Defeating the Stalker reveals the path to the canopy bridge' — defeat-triggered, specific. Enc 1 'Capturing the monkey's spirit opens the boss gate' — capture action (appropriate for a spirit battle), specific but bypasses encounter structure. Enc 2 'Exorcising the Jaguar reveals the Wraith's altar' — defeat-equivalent, specific. Triggers are specific but enc 1 skips directly to 'boss gate' rather than the next encounter.

balance.reason: Health escalates 40-60-80 and damage 10-15-20. Boss at 150/25 strictly harder. Total incoming (10+15+20+25=70) vs 100 HP — appropriate for medium. Jungle Nectar (minor health restore) from enc 2 provides minimal healing. Clean escalation.

thematic_coherence.reason: Mist-shrouded prehistoric jungle, Vine Stalker, Spirit Monkey, Shadow Jaguar, Chrono-Wraith — the chronomancer elements (Chronos Shard, Temporal Siphon) blend with jungle eerie appropriately. 'Ancient Ziggurat Entrance' is a slightly generic location name.

mechanical_sense.reason: Chronos Shard (increases attack speed) against a Spirit Monkey — time-acceleration making the monkey vulnerable to rapid strikes is logically coherent for a chronomancer. Temporal Siphon (extracts temporal energy) against a Shadow Jaguar and Chrono-Wraith — draining time energy from temporal entities is perfectly aligned.

completeness.reason: Locations are distinct. Enemies are varied. All pickups have specific effects. Jungle Nectar's 'minor health restoration' effect is vague but functional. 'Control over the jungle's flow of time' reward is thematically resonant.

judgment.best_element: The mechanical sense is flawless — Chronos Shard and Temporal Siphon create a chronomancer-specific toolkit that logically connects to each enemy type through temporal energy mechanics.
judgment.worst_element: Encounter 1 trigger 'Capturing the monkey's spirit opens the boss gate' — this implies opening the final boss gate at encounter 1, skipping the enc 2 → boss narrative structure.
judgment.overall: A well-referenced and mechanically sound medium-difficulty level; the enc 1 trigger's 'boss gate' phrasing is a structural awkwardness, but the level is otherwise shippable.

judgment.summary: A desolate brutal jungle berserker level with a notable mechanic: encounter 2 enemy (Alpha Stalker) is weak to 'Charged Obsidian Spike', and the encounter 2 pickup IS 'Charged Obsidian Spike'. This is a self-referential loop where the player needs the item to defeat the enemy but gets it from the same encounter. Boss correctly uses it.

reference_integrity.reason: Enc 0 weakness 'Iron Greataxe' (player weapon) exact. Enc 1 weakness 'Obsidian Spike' (enc 0 pickup) exact. Enc 2 weakness 'Charged Obsidian Spike' (enc 2 pickup — same encounter) — this creates a self-referential loop. Boss weakness 'Charged Obsidian Spike' exact. The enc 2 self-reference is unusual but enc 2's trigger implies the encounter 2 pickup charges the spike: the pickup IS the charged version.

causal_chain.reason: Enc 0 trigger 'Dying scream alerts the pack guardians ahead' — defeat-triggered but 'dying scream' implies the enemy activates the trigger with its death cry, which is specific. Enc 1 'Breaking the ruin altar summons the Alpha Stalker' — player action (breaking an altar), not a defeat consequence. Enc 2 'Defeat leads to the opening of the inner sanctum gate' — generic ('defeat leads to'). Two weak triggers.

balance.reason: Health escalates 80-120-200 and damage 15-25-40. Boss at 400/50 strictly harder. Total incoming (15+25+40+50=130) vs 150 HP — very tight for brutal with only Storm Essence and Charged Obsidian Spike (no healing). Survivable with Storm Essence's lightning boost helping kill faster, but no direct healing.

thematic_coherence.reason: Desolate jungle ruins, Rotting Vine-Strangler, Overgrown Guardian, Alpha Stalker, Primeval Blight-Stalker — all fit desolate primeval dread. Obsidian Spike, Storm Essence, Charged Obsidian Spike — the escalating spike concept is a nice mechanical throughline.

mechanical_sense.reason: Obsidian Spike (conducts electrical energy) against an Overgrown Guardian — electrifying a plant/stone guardian makes sense. Charged Obsidian Spike (storm-charged) against an Alpha Stalker and Primeval Blight-Stalker — storm energy vs. parasitic blight is thematically coherent.

completeness.reason: Enc 2 trigger 'Defeat leads to the opening of the inner sanctum gate' is generic and starts with 'Defeat leads to' which is minimal. Enc 1 pickup 'Storm Essence' and its effect on the boss is indirect (fights via enc 2 pickup). No healing pickup available.

judgment.best_element: The Obsidian Spike → Charged Obsidian Spike progression creates a two-stage crafting narrative within the level — finding the spike and then charging it before the final fight.
judgment.worst_element: Encounter 1 trigger 'Breaking the ruin altar summons the Alpha Stalker' is a player action rather than a defeat consequence — the encounter 1 enemy's defeat doesn't logically cause enc 2 to appear.
judgment.overall: Creative weapon-crafting narrative with good thematic consistency, but two non-defeat triggers and the self-referential enc 2 pickup loop need design clarification before shipping.
